input.background: This fictitious but realistic example considers odds ratios from a case-control study on asbestos and lung cancer while considering the concurrent effects of smoking.
input.question: Whenever a statistical interaction exists, Mantel-Haenszel summary statistics should not be calculated.

output.answer: Instead, strata-specific statistics should be reported.

asbestos_exposure.value: Presence or absence of asbestos exposure (categorical values such as 'exposed' or 'not exposed')
asbestos_exposure.description: Indicates whether an individual was exposed to asbestos.

lung_cancer_status.value: Presence or absence of lung cancer (categorical values such as 'case' or 'control')
lung_cancer_status.description: Indicates whether an individual has lung cancer (case) or not (control).

smoking_status.value: Smoking behavior of individuals (categorical values such as 'smoker' or 'non-smoker')
smoking_status.description: Indicates whether an individual is a smoker or a non-smoker.

odds_ratios.value: Odds ratios calculated for the association between asbestos exposure, smoking, and lung cancer
odds_ratios.description: Odds ratios used to measure the strength of association between variables in the case-control study.

statistical_interaction.value: Existence or absence of interaction effects between asbestos exposure and smoking on lung cancer risk
statistical_interaction.description: Indicates whether there is a statistical interaction between asbestos exposure and smoking in their effect on lung cancer.

meta_info.caption: Statistical interaction (Asbestos, cigarettes, and lung cancer).
